identity.title: Define versioned core contracts and executable state-machine specifications

authorization.reference: Operator explicitly ordered activation and implementation of WP-FF-005, reuse of existing research, implementation assistance by subagents, and multi-lens adversarial review of the implementation followed by remediation and hardening.

lifecycle.next_action: Merge closure commit acf5465518e94984848e7d1f725575a99daebd55 and this MERGE_PENDING evidence record to main, then mechanically verify containment before VALIDATED.

scope.summary: Freeze the Phase 0B serializable product and diagnostic contracts, identity relationships, bounded resource models, and owned state-machine specifications needed before broad implementation.
in_scope: Versioned serializable source graph, item/representation/track/asset/derived-output identities, provenance, tri-state validity, configuration, event, error, cancellation, and public JSON contracts.
Versioned process, plugin IPC, JavaScript-worker IPC, and diagnostic envelope contracts with limits, request IDs, compatibility ranges, errors, cancellation acknowledgement, and provenance.
Atomic multi-resource admission and end-to-end byte-credit models with item/byte bounds, fairness, cancellation, and durable-position semantics.

Executable transition specifications for job/cancel, source/redirect, admission, fragment/durability, live, sink, FFmpeg, JavaScript, plugin, commit/archive, filesystem-capability, and watcher lifecycles.
Versioning, compatibility, migration, serialization round-trip, malformed-input, and cross-version fixture policies.
Machine-readable contract inventory mapping every contract/state transition to stable IDs, owners, invariants, proof methods, and readiness gates.
No-context model manual updates for contract inspection, fixture replay, model tests, and schema-change recovery.
non_goals: Implementing concrete network, extractor, storage, FFmpeg, JavaScript, plugin, scheduler, or watcher behavior beyond narrow contract/model-test scaffolding.
Selecting an archive store, JavaScript engine, transport backend, or new runtime/native dependency.
Freezing compatibility behavior not grounded in the pinned profile/corpus.
Treating example Rust types in the design as complete normative implementations.
Claiming liveness, durability, or cancellation safety without executable model and failure-prefix evidence.
dependencies: WP-FF-004 compatibility profile/corpus schemas or an explicitly provisional compatibility mapping
Accepted coarse rings and crate-boundary rules in design Sections 8.5 and 9
FF-BUILD-010 through FF-BUILD-035 invariance, effect-boundary, backpressure, and termination rules

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Every required Phase 0B contract has a stable ID, schema/version policy, owner, documented limits/errors, compatibility range, serialization fixture, and machine-readable inventory entry; no runtime handles, Tokio types, sockets, processes, or runtime trait objects occur in contract crates.
evidence: build/fixtures/contracts/inventory.json enumerates 20 bounded data-only contracts and 12 pure state machines with stable IDs, owners, schemas, limits, errors, fixtures, invariants, and proof bindings.
fforager-contracts 32/32 tests and fforager-testkit 10/10 tests pass, including inventory digest, canonical fixture decode/validation, and semantic mutation rejection.
FF-GATE-ARCH-001 records a successful recursive forbidden-handle scan, dependency-boundary checks, and exact inventory/manual/adversarial evidence checks.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Identity contracts distinguish item, representation, track, asset, and derived output, preserve provenance and relationships across serialization, and reject collisions, ambiguous canonicalization, and archive-ineligible partial outputs.
evidence: fforager-contracts graph, identity, protocol, framing, and storage tests pass 32/32.
Typed IDs reject wrong prefixes/case; graph validation rejects duplicate or dangling roots, provenance and overlay cycles, ambiguous redirects, and archive-ineligible partial output.
Canonical public fixtures round-trip and recursively validate through fforager-testkit.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Each named state machine declares states, owner, preconditions, invariants, postconditions, cancellation outcomes, durable prefixes, invalid transitions, and finite-model assumptions; model/property tests cover success, failure, cancellation, restart, and counterfactual invalid transitions.
evidence: fforager-core lifecycle/resource tests pass 29/29 and fforager-testkit executes success, failure, cancellation, restart, and invalid-transition cases.
Lifecycle acknowledgements bind lifecycle, generation, and nonzero machine instance; stale, wrong-generation, cross-instance, transient-restore, and unacknowledged-success paths fail closed.
The inventory binds every named state machine to owner, states, invariants, invalid transitions, durable prefixes, cancellation outcomes, finite assumptions, and executable proof IDs.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Atomic resource-vector and byte-credit models have checked arithmetic, item/byte ceilings, admission/release invariants, no permit leaks, bounded cancellation progress, and saturation tests demonstrating bounded state under stalled consumers.
evidence: fforager-core resource tests cover zero, exact, one-over, checked overflow/underflow, atomic release, FIFO head reservation, bounded queue bytes/items, cancellation dispatch, and owner isolation.
Byte-credit receive requires exact claim and owner, preserves consumed attribution, revokes unused released credit, rejects consumed-claim transfer, and prevents durability beyond credited bytes.
All 13 resource dimensions are represented in the executable inventory/fixture proof surface.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Cross-version fixtures prove documented current/current and supported compatibility outcomes, while unknown mandatory kinds, incompatible major versions, malformed/oversized frames, partial frames, duplicate IDs, and illegal transitions fail with typed bounded errors.
evidence: Canonical current/supported fixtures decode and validate; trusted local bindings reject self-declared schema/version identities.

Framing tests reject partial headers/payloads, oversized frames before payload allocation, unknown mandatory kinds, duplicate requests, incompatible versions, invalid nested envelopes, and uncorrelated cancellation acknowledgements with typed bounded errors.
Extension and tagged-enum deserialization reject unknown or over-budget fields, and inventory semantic mutations fail closed.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: The canonical architecture, PR, and triggered deep gates pass; reports map every acceptance row and readiness-gate contract to concrete tests and residual uncertainty.
evidence: PASS FF-GATE-ARCH-001: build/reports/architecture-check-1784496901614013300-642088.json; source commit acf5465518e94984848e7d1f725575a99daebd55, dirty=false.

FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 report build/reports/runtime-truth-check-1784496945148219800-630752.json preserves the exact non-product prerequisite ceiling and makes no runtime completion claim; source commit acf5465518e94984848e7d1f725575a99daebd55, dirty=false.

PASS FF-GATE-DEEP-001: build/reports/verify-deep-1784497031416014200-644600.json; source commit acf5465518e94984848e7d1f725575a99daebd55, dirty=false; 32 contract, 29 core, 13 diagnostics, 10 testkit, and 58 xtask tests plus all-feature doctests pass.

FF-GATE-PR-001 aggregate report build/reports/verify-pr-1784497118648624100-674796.json records successful formatting, warnings-denied Clippy, tests, rustdoc, dependency policy, architecture, prerequisite-ceiling, and proof checks; source commit acf5465518e94984848e7d1f725575a99daebd55, dirty=false.

acceptance_matrix.requirement: The Phase 0 model manual documents contract locations, schemas, versioning rules, inspection commands, model tests, fixture regeneration, safety limits, common failures, and recovery without requiring chat history.
evidence: product/MODEL_MANUAL.md documents contract locations, trusted schema/framing boundaries, exact locked inspection/test commands, safety limits, acknowledgements, durable restore rules, resource ownership, inventory integrity, failure diagnosis, and recovery.
The manual validator and manual review record a successful result while preserving the explicit non-product prerequisite ceiling.

Independent adversarial implementation reviews reached PASS after remediation across wire, lifecycle, resource, instance, restore, attribution, fixture, inventory, scanner, progress, disposition, deep-composition, and inventory-gate lenses.

not_proven: No Ferric execution capability or adapter behavior is proven
Production network, extractor, storage, FFmpeg, JavaScript, plugin, scheduler, watcher, packaging, or release behavior
Cross-platform runtime behavior outside the pure deterministic contract/model proof surface
End-to-end liveness, durability, cancellation, and compatibility through a future staged production artifact
Release readiness or yt-dlp replacement parity

non_product_prerequisite.required_future_proof: FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 through the exact staged production artifact

repository_layout.runtime_boundary: Execution from product packages must require neither .GOV/ nor build/.

required_outcomes: Versioned data-only product and diagnostic boundaries reject incompatible, malformed, ambiguous, oversized, and uncorrelated inputs.
Pure lifecycle and resource models distinguish effect intent from acknowledged durable state and preserve ownership across cancellation, restart, and release.
Canonical fixtures, inventory rows, compiled tests, and gate reports bind every Phase 0B prerequisite claim to executable proof.
The prerequisite ceiling is the exact non_product_prerequisite declaration in this packet.
risk_cases: A sender self-authorizes a schema version, smuggles an unknown field, omits correlation identity, or bypasses framing limits.
A stale acknowledgement, transient restored state, released credit, or smaller queued request creates false success, ownership loss, or starvation.
An inventory, manual, adversarial record, or prerequisite wording mutation creates a false PASS without executable evidence.
A runtime handle enters a data-only crate through an alias, grouped import, standard stream, process, socket, or external pipe type.

reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.candidate: no mechanism beyond the accepted outcome
reuse_candidates_in_order_with_result.result: Rejected because stable public boundaries, executable lifecycle models, and proof gates are explicit Phase 0B deliverables.
Selected for the existing workspace, diagnostics contract, architecture gate, compatibility schemas, reports, and fixture conventions.
Selected for checked arithmetic, deterministic collections, typed state transitions, bounded framing, and data-only ownership models.
Selected for locked Cargo compilation, Clippy, rustdoc, Git identity, filesystem containment, and process-bounded gate execution.
Existing serde, serde_json, sha2, and build-tool dependencies cover serialization, hashing, fixtures, and validation without a new runtime dependency.
Selected as three data-only/pure crates, one testkit, canonical fixtures, one inventory, and extensions to the existing xtask gate.

artifact_specific_justification.product_contracts: Own stable typed identities, graph relationships, acquisition and sink data, process boundaries, storage descriptors, and bounded validation.
artifact_specific_justification.diagnostics_contracts: Own transport-independent diagnostic, lifecycle, health, sequence, privacy, and negotiation data without runtime handles.
artifact_specific_justification.core_models: Own pure effect-intent state transitions, acknowledged durable prefixes, admission ownership, fairness, and byte-credit conservation.
artifact_specific_justification.conformance_fixtures: Provide canonical current, prior, incompatible, malformed, boundary, lifecycle, resource, and durability inputs replayed by compiled tests.
artifact_specific_justification.inventory_gate: Binds stable contract and state IDs to exact owners, Rust types, fixtures, invariants, readiness gates, and executable proof IDs.
artifact_specific_justification.model_manual: Lets a no-context model inspect, change, validate, and recover the Phase 0B contracts without relying on conversation history.

change_evidence.pre_simplification_verdict: PASS: the initial implementation exposed duplicated boundary assumptions, unacknowledged effect transitions, weak ownership attribution, and proof surfaces that could accept fabricated evidence.
simplification_changes: Centralized trusted schema/type validation and recursive framing limits instead of letting each sender self-declare compatibility.
Represented pending effects and acknowledgements with explicit effect and generation identity instead of treating a generic acknowledgement as success.
Used strict FIFO head reservation and claim-owner byte attribution instead of implicit queue bypass and global credit consumption.
Bound inventory rows and adversarial dispositions to exact canonical executable proofs instead of lexical proof-shaped strings.
Composed PR verification from the actual shared deep checks and exact locked manual commands instead of parallel approximations.
change_evidence.post_simplification_verdict: PASS: the final design has explicit trusted boundaries, correlated state effects, auditable ownership, canonical fixtures, and one fail-closed deep/PR proof composition.
complete_post_change_verification: Parse every changed JSON and canonical YAML surface, run git diff --check, and verify the closure commit is contained in main.

ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.current_ceiling: These artifacts prove data contracts and pure prerequisite models only; they implement no operator-usable network, storage, FFmpeg, plugin, JavaScript, watcher, packaging, or release capability.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.replacement_trigger: A later adapter-backed packet changes impact classification and supplies FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 evidence.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.likely_upgrade_path: Bind these contracts to real adapters and execute success, negative, and counterfactual scenarios through FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001.
ceilings_exceptions_and_evidence.trigger_detection_method: Taskboard activation, packet impact classification, member inventory, changed-path classification, and strict runtime-proof manifest validation identify the later trigger.

DIFF_ATTACK_SURFACES: Contract and serialization boundaries: schema identity, compatibility, unknown fields, framing, provenance, graph cycles, and extension budgets.
State and resource boundaries: effect acknowledgement, durable restoration, cancellation, FIFO admission, byte-credit ownership, and atomic release.
Integration and fixture boundaries: real Rust types, lifecycle negative paths, all resource dimensions, durability DTOs, and canonical inventory mappings.
Gate boundaries: runtime-handle aliases, zero-progress wording, executable proof IDs, manual commands, doctests, deep composition, and review dispositions.
INDEPENDENT_CHECKS_RUN: Three rotated reviewers independently inspected successive immutable remediation commits with wire, core, fixture, gate, manual, and closure-evidence lenses.
Workspace proof results: formatting and compilation accepted; 32 contract, 29 core, 13 diagnostic, 10 testkit, and 58 xtask tests accepted.
Warnings-denied Clippy ran across every workspace target and feature after combined remediation.
Architecture, runtime-truth, deep, and PR gates were rerun from the active taskboard packet and their emitted reports inspected.
Mutation probes exercised stale acknowledgements, transient restore, wrong-owner credits, schema self-authorization, unknown fields, import aliases, fabricated proofs, and completion wording.
COUNTERFACTUAL_CHECKS: Removing trusted schema/type binding or recursive validation causes cross-type, unsupported-version, unknown-field, and over-limit probes to fail the expected oracle.
Supplying a stale effect generation, wrong effect, transient restore state, or wrong credit owner is rejected without mutating model state.
Replacing a canonical inventory proof, owner, readiness gate, state set, invariant, or Rust type causes compiled conformance validation to fail.
Reintroducing runtime-handle aliases, positive prerequisite completion claims, contradictory review dispositions, duplicate manual commands, or omitted deep checks causes gate tests to fail.
BOUNDARY_PROBES: Bounded frame bytes through Serde and concrete process, plugin, JavaScript-worker, event, error, config, and cancellation validators.
Source graph roots, provenance chains, redirect overlays, aggregate identity counts, tri-state fields, and namespaced extensions.
Lifecycle effect intents through correlated acknowledgements, durable restart prefixes, cancellation drains, and invalid transition replay.
Resource requests and byte claims through capacity, owner caps, FIFO reservation, consumption attribution, transfer, release, and durability positions.
Packet, inventory, manual, source scanner, doctest, deep, and PR producers through their exact fail-closed consumers.
NEGATIVE_PATH_CHECKS: Malformed, oversized, partial, duplicate, unknown-kind, incompatible-version, cross-schema, traversal, cycle, and unnamespaced-field inputs are rejected.
Stale or wrong acknowledgements, transient restore, failure, cancellation, restart, illegal transitions, and false durable prefixes are rejected or explicitly modeled.
Over-capacity vectors, queue saturation, wrong-owner release or consumption, released unused credit, arithmetic overflow, and large-request starvation are covered.
Fake proof IDs, wrong owners or readiness gates, missing fixture fields, runtime-handle import evasions, progress synonyms, contradictory findings, and manual command stuffing are rejected.

INDEPENDENT_FINDINGS.finding: Trusted recursive validation rejects schema self-authorization, incomplete framed boundaries, permissive nested fields, and missing graph or worker bounds.
Effect intents, restart prefixes, and cancellation drains now require the exact pending effect and generation acknowledgement before durable advancement.
Admission starvation and cross-owner byte-credit consumption were closed with strict FIFO reservation and claim-owner attribution receipts.
Cross-instance acknowledgement routing was closed by binding every acknowledgement to a nonzero stable machine instance, exact effect, and generation.
Lifecycle restoration now accepts only the exact durable-state whitelist cross-checked against the canonical inventory.
Consumed byte claims can no longer transfer ownership and rewrite historical receive attribution.
Every canonical inventory field is line-ending-portably digest-bound and representative semantic mutations fail validation.
Lifecycle, resource, and durability fixtures now execute success, failure, cancellation, restart, invalid, all-dimension, and ownership cases.
Scanner mutations reject direct, grouped, globbed, nested, and aliased standard stream or external pipe imports.
Prerequisite completion claims under nested runtime, product, capability, release, packaging, or phase subjects now fail closed.
Adversarial finding dispositions now require stable finding identities and exact finding-specific executable proof mappings.
All-feature doctests and the real shared deep-check composition now execute inside both deep and PR verification.
Inventory identifiers, owners, readiness gates, Rust types, states, invariants, and executable proof mappings are exact and mutation-tested.

RESIDUAL_UNCERTAINTY: Adapter execution, peer authentication, operating-system confinement, and child-process cleanup are outside this prerequisite and require later FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 proof.
Strict FIFO deliberately accepts head-of-line blocking as the bounded anti-starvation policy; later scheduling may require a separately specified reservation or aging policy.

The lexical data-only scanner is backed by exact demonstrated alias mutations and architecture boundaries, but code review remains necessary for novel macro-generated or dependency-internal handle paths.

MANUAL_REVIEW.reviewer: Codex primary with three rotated independent implementation reviewers
MANUAL_REVIEW.method: Read the exact contract-operation topic against source, inventory, fixtures, commands, safety limits, failure recovery, and prerequisite ceiling after final remediation.
evidence: The manual contains the six exact locked Cargo workflows required for focused contract, core, diagnostic, testkit, Clippy, and deep validation.
The manual documents typed failure recovery for framing, schema, lifecycle, resource, inventory, and data-only scan failures without relying on chat history.
The manual assigns later adapter execution evidence to FF-GATE-RUNTIME-001 and preserves the prerequisite ceiling.

stub_implementation_brief.base_scope: Turn Phase 0B's product/public/process/diagnostic contracts and lifecycle promises into versioned Rust data plus executable transition and invariant proof surfaces.

implementation_readiness_gate_items: Accepted public behavior, dependency, security, identity, durability, and performance decisions
Versioned serializable source graph, identity, plugin IPC, event, error, cancellation, and process protocols
Atomic resource-vector and byte-credit invariants with executable saturation and cancellation tests
Commit/archive/resume fault injection across every durable state prefix
Executable phase gates with machine-readable reports
No-context model manual, structured diagnostics, stable identifiers, and parallel-agent-safe receipts

promotion_requirements: Inspect WP-003 architecture/tooling output and WP-004 profile/corpus schemas before freezing physical paths or compatibility fields.

Research current primary-source Rust serialization/schema evolution, protocol framing, model/property testing, cancellation, resource accounting, and crash-safe state-machine patterns; record inspected code/tests and rejected options.
Create a clause-to-contract/state-machine map for only the touched design clauses and assign each invariant to a proof method.
Decide which state models are pure core functions and which effect boundaries remain adapter ports; hidden ambient state is forbidden.
planned_outputs: Versioned Rust product and diagnostic contract crates
Pure transition models with property/model tests
Atomic resource-vector and byte-credit model tests
Malformed, oversized, incompatible, and invalid-transition negative fixtures

high_roi_additions.addition: Generate a contract index and compatibility matrix from the contract inventory.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Stable IDs, owners, versions, and fixtures are already required, so a generated view is cheap and prevents manual drift.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Makes contract discovery and cross-version support legible to humans and no-context models.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Contract inventory, WP-003 report envelope, and rustdoc metadata.
high_roi_additions.verification: Generation is deterministic and fails when a public contract lacks inventory metadata or a fixture.

high_roi_additions.addition: Create one reusable conformance harness for framed process protocols.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Plugin, JavaScript, worker, and watcher protocols share framing, versioning, bounds, request IDs, cancellation, and malformed-input needs.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Avoids duplicated parsers and inconsistent security behavior across later adapters.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Shared contract types, testkit, fixture conventions, and negative probes.
high_roi_additions.verification: Run the same partial/oversized/duplicate/incompatible/cancel cases against every registered framed protocol.

high_roi_additions.addition: Add schema-change diff and compatibility-budget checks.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Version and fixture metadata already exists; comparing it at PR time prevents accidental breaking changes before consumers multiply.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Reduces future migration and watcher/plugin compatibility rework.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Canonical prior fixtures, contract inventory, and xtask reporting.
high_roi_additions.verification: Breaking fixture/schema mutation fails unless a stable accepted decision and version transition are present.

high_roi_additions.addition: Expose deterministic transition traces and replay seeds in machine-readable test reports.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Model tests already produce transitions; retaining compact traces makes concurrency/durability failures reproducible for parallel agents.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents non-reproducible state-machine failures and chat-only debugging context.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Stable state/event IDs, WP-003 report format, and testkit controlled clocks/randomness.
high_roi_additions.verification: A reported failing seed/trace replays to the same verdict on the pinned toolchain.

existing_systems_to_reuse: WP-003 Cargo workspace, xtask, architecture policy, reports, and negative fixtures
WP-004 compatibility profile, stable oracle IDs, normalization, and fixtures
Accepted coarse contract/core/testkit crates from design Sections 8.5 and 9
Build-rule invariance, pure-core, backpressure, termination, and coding-discipline gates
gaps_closed: Persisted source and identity models become serializable and versioned.
Lifecycle prose becomes owned executable transition models.
Resource admission and byte flow gain atomic bounded invariants.
Process/public/diagnostic boundaries gain common versioning, limits, and typed failure semantics.
Later adapters can implement against stable ports instead of inventing incompatible contracts.

risk_scenarios.risk: Contracts freeze guessed behavior too early.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Later corpus or spike evidence forces pervasive schema breakage or compatibility hacks.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Ground compatibility fields in WP-004, separate typed stable identity/security fields from bounded namespaced extensions, and mark provisional fields explicitly until evidence closes them.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Trace every compatibility-facing field to a design clause or corpus row and reject unreferenced public behavior.

risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Contracts become tied to Tokio, filesystem handles, sockets, processes, or concrete adapters and cannot serve IPC/persistence consumers.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Architecture/source scans enforce data-only contract crates; runtime cursors and effect handles live behind core/adapter ports.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Negative fixtures add prohibited types/dependencies and must fail architecture-check.

risk_scenarios.risk: State machines omit failure prefixes or cancellation races.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Happy-path tests pass while restart, duplicate commit, permit leak, or partial-frame states violate durability or liveness.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Enumerate every durable prefix and invalid transition, use pure transitions plus model/property tests, inject cancellation at every transition, and retain replay traces.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Mutation/counterfactual tests remove one transition guard or release and the deep gate must detect it.

risk_scenarios.risk: Resource accounting overflows or admits coupled resources non-atomically.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Concurrent jobs each pass separate semaphores but exceed aggregate memory/disk/process limits or leak credits on cancellation.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Use checked arithmetic and one atomic resource vector with structured ownership/release; prove saturation and cancel paths under controlled schedules.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Boundary tests cover zero, exact capacity, one-over, near-integer-limit, stalled consumer, cancellation, and repeated acquire/release.

risk_scenarios.risk: Version compatibility is asserted from round-trip tests only.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Current code reads its own output while prior/minor/unknown-major or partial frames fail unpredictably.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Retain canonical prior wire fixtures, explicit compatibility ranges, unknown-field policies, and independent cross-version readers/writers.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Current/current, supported prior/current, unknown-compatible-minor, incompatible-major, malformed, and oversized matrices all emit expected typed outcomes.

verification_needs: Architecture scans proving data-only contract crates and inward dependency direction
Serialization round-trip plus canonical prior/current cross-version fixtures
Property/model tests with deterministic replay traces
Saturation, checked-arithmetic, cancellation, and permit-leak probes
Malformed, partial, oversized, duplicate, reordered, unknown-kind, and incompatible-version protocol probes
Mutation tests for transition guards and invariants
no_context_execution_order: Read startup authority, taskboard, this active packet, topology, build rules, and cited design/readiness anchors.
Confirm promotion from STUB, WP-003 validation, WP-004 dependency status, and current Git checkpoint.
Complete current research and contract/state-machine clause mapping before changing code.
Define inventory metadata, version policy, pure transition ownership, limits, invariants, and fixtures before implementations.
Implement smallest data-only contracts and pure state models; reuse one framing/conformance harness.
Run architecture, serialization, cross-version, model, saturation, cancellation, mutation, and malformed-input proof.
Update manual and packet proof; merge only after canonical gates and independent adversarial validation pass.
